questions.question: The main idea developed in the text is :

propositions.A: The balance between technology and nature
propositions.B: The benefits and drawbacks of technology

questions.justification: The whole passage is built around the 'double edged sword' idea: it details technology's advantages (paragraphs 1-4) then its downsides (paragraphs 5-6). A and D only cover part of that scope.

questions.question: This (also leads to physical) :
questions.justification: The sentence reads: 'People are suffering from anxiety and phobia due to excessive usage of multimedia technology. This also leads to...' — 'This' picks up the cause just mentioned, i.e. the excessive usage itself.

questions.question: their (depletion) :
questions.justification: '...consume a lot of natural resources and power gradually leading to their depletion' — 'their' agrees with the plural noun phrase immediately preceding it.

questions.question: it (is required) :
questions.justification: 'Manufacturing technology creates a huge amount of E-waste and proper disposal of it is required...' — 'it' is the object that needs disposing of, i.e. the E-waste.

questions.question: Its (: Its potential) :
questions.justification: 'Its potential is limitless and awaits great discoveries' continues the sentence about technology introduced earlier in paragraph 1.

questions.question: it (becomes very important to strike...) :
questions.justification: This 'it' is a dummy/impersonal subject ('it becomes important to...'), like in 'it is raining' — it does not refer back to any specific noun in the text.

questions.question: Match the first paragraph to the given titles :
questions.justification: Paragraph 1 lists technology's everyday conveniences (education, communication, medicine, transportation, sports) — exactly what D names.

questions.question: According to the text, it is possible to obtain a balance between technology and nature.

propositions.D: The text implies it but does not state it explicitly

questions.justification: The conclusion states explicitly: 'Judicious and decisive use of technology could help in creating a balance between technology and nature.'

questions.question: According to the text, technology moves very fast and is mutable.

propositions.D: The text implies it but does not state it explicitly

questions.justification: Paragraph 1 states directly: 'Technology is fast paced and ever changing. Today's technology in no time becomes obsolete.'

questions.question: In paragraph 4, a drawback of the use of technology in the medicine sector is given.

propositions.D: The text implies it but does not state it explicitly

questions.justification: Paragraph 4 ends with: 'technology has resulted in focusing and treating only on the sick part of the body rather than caring for the patient as a whole' — an explicit drawback.

questions.question: In the text, a broad definition of technology is provided.

propositions.D: The text implies it but does not state it explicitly

questions.justification: The text describes technology's effects and uses extensively but never formally defines the term itself.

questions.question: "Technology can be regarded as a double edged sword" means :

propositions.B: Technology has impacts on our past and our future
propositions.D: Technology affects our society both positively as well as negatively

questions.justification: A double-edged sword is a classic idiom for something that can cut both ways — here, technology's benefits and harms, matching D.

questions.question: "Technology is advancing with a speed of light" means :

propositions.D: Technology is advancing thanks to scientists

questions.justification: 'Speed of light' is a figurative way of saying 'extremely fast', matching option C.

questions.question: "Today's technology in no time becomes obsolete" means :

propositions.A: Today's technology becomes obsolete without time
propositions.B: Today's technology becomes obsolete very quickly
propositions.C: Today's technology becomes obsolete with time
propositions.D: Today's technology becomes obsolete very simply

questions.justification: 'In no time' is an idiom meaning 'very quickly', not literally 'without any time passing'.

questions.question: "Balance between technology and nature is the need of the hour" means :

propositions.A: balance between technology and nature is urgent
propositions.B: balance between technology and nature has many advantages
propositions.C: balance between technology and nature is useless
propositions.D: balance between technology and nature has limits

questions.justification: 'The need of the hour' is an idiom meaning something urgently required right now.

questions.question: "everything is a miracle of technology", this idea is also developed in :
questions.justification: The phrase appears verbatim in paragraph 4, but the same sentiment — everyday objects being part of technology's 'miracle' — is echoed in paragraph 2: 'everything is the whimsical world of technology.'

questions.question: "People are suffering from anxiety", this idea is also developed in :
questions.justification: The sentence is from paragraph 6; the same mental-health concern (stress, depression from isolation) is already introduced in paragraph 5.

questions.question: "It becomes very important to strike a balance between technology and nature", this idea is also developed in :
questions.justification: The sentence is from paragraph 5; the conclusion (paragraph 7) restates it: 'Striking a balance between technology and nature is the need of the hour.'

questions.question: Various :
questions.justification: The noun form of the adjective 'various' is 'variety'. B and C are not real English words.

questions.question: Improved :
questions.justification: The noun form of 'improved/improve' is 'improvement'. 'Improvisation' is a different word entirely (related to 'improvise').
